## [00:00 - 03:02]

So now you understand what the core for is I want to show you how I use the four pillars of the core for product audience offer and funnel to analyze where my product and brand and service is right now and Where I need to focus my attention now if you've never run ads before The core for grading can help you understand where your biggest problems are and where your biggest levers are if you have run ads before It will define where you need to optimize and work on before you try and scale up and if I've seen One big problem. It's that people will often come to me and say I want to scale up And I can't but there are so many other things that need to be fixed first that they're not worried about Fixing but they just want to spend more and make more and there are steps that go into place before you're able to do that and do that Consistently so in this section, I'll explain the advertising core for school chart I'll show you how to grade your AC for some case studies and benchmarks and then creating your AC for action plan as well So here's the template that we use and you will see a link below for you to Make your own version of this as well and what I do is Whenever I'm launching my own e-com offers my own info products or if we take on client clients in the agency What we're doing is to find a baseline for where they are right now on their core for So with their products we look at how well the product is positioned in the market for their offer How well the proposition is constructed for the audience? The ability to identify and find your perfect perfect customer and then funnel is how well you're able to convert that user Into a sale or a leader or whatever it is and then we score that from five per section So five out of five five out of five would give you 20 out of five and then 20 out of 20 And then any comments are going here on observations as well What we then do is to look at the score and this is based on auditing You know countless hundreds hundreds 500 plus I don't know we've audited so many ad accounts with a core for and we found a familiar pattern if this score overall out of 20 comes between If you know we score out one to five if the score is 20 it means it's absolutely perfect So all of the scores are perfect I don't think we've ever seen this But I would say that we have developed ad accounts that have enabled to get to that position Because they they are the ones that really have a product that absolutely cuts through they absolutely understand the perfect customer their offers are on point and their funnels convert like crazy when you are in this position you're in the perfect position to scow because your Business your marketing your advertising everything is aligned with Facebook and Facebook it will give you what you want from the auction a score between 15 and 19 which is a lot Businesses that are ready to scale Means that you can still do some optimization on your core for but you should be in a position to

## [03:02 - 06:02]

Start scaling because you should be getting a lot more stability etc. etc If you score between 10 and 14 It's a bit too early to scale up It means that one or two parts or maybe more of your pillars aren't as optimized as possible So it could be that your offer isn't strong that your click-through rates are low your conversion rates are low You're you know, you're not getting the profitability your row as the targets, etc. Just not being met Then you need to do more optimization and testing if your score is below nine and I have unfortunately Unfortunately seen at accounts where it does score below nine you have a lot more work to do than worrying about Facebook ads You'd probably need to review your products itself Do you really understand your avatar your offers probably poor and your website's probably poor? So if you're in that position you need to really work on more than just your Facebook advertising so Going back to that we kind of tell you through those scores get an idea of kind of where we are and then we create a plan So I want to share with you our three examples based on Audits that we've conducted to give you an idea of how we construct that score So at this stage what I would recommend you do is to get that spreadsheet open So that you can actually put your scores in whilst you're understanding what goes into Our process and how we actually score in grade now one thing I would also recommend is quite often when we do this in the agency We get two or three people to grade the Core for that means that we take the average of everyone and we're not using an opinion We look at the average across so this is an e-com electronic store And I believe three people inputted including myself and my team on Different parts of the funnel so how well was a product positioned in the market? We get we've given it a score and we've also made some comments So this product was well-rounded a lot of uses good average order value Meaning extra room for and CPA for example can work well with bundling high chance of selling more than one unit multiple Targets options available and so variants results from October and for this particular client show there is a large interest So the product was well positioned it clearly solved a problem or created new opportunities So we gave it a five out of five then on the offer side We only gave it a two and this was a big area to improve There's a need to generate new angles for the product through testing need to fix the landing page and brand fills So let's drop shipy So this was a client that wanted to take their drop ship store into a brand and bring products over from China to the US for example and their current ads and product pages were looking quite desperate like we really want to sell there's Timers and conversion things which were just making the Experience look really bad and it could tell you could tell from the conversion rate and looking at user replays Then we looked at the audience We had a lot of data on interest lookalikes targeting Didn't seem to be a big issue, but there were still untapped audiences

## [06:02 - 09:07]

So they did a good job of testing and lots and lots of audiences, but the messaging was really poor So we felt like we had good sales data I think they had maybe 10,000 sales for example, but they weren't as profitable as they need to be So this is another key thing. It's easy to generate sales in Facebook But to be profitable hit your KPIs and etc You can have to work hard in your offer and also your funnel So we scored the funnel or three Conversion rate was okay at 2.5% But there was still so much opportunity to be had to improve conversion rate and also average order value The product itself cost about 40 maybe 50 dollars And and with that there's two things I would focus on is that conversion rate could be a lot better because it is a low ticket product and also bumping up with bundles and other opportunities to push that average order value up would mean that We can get a higher original First purchase the value from them from that 50 to 50 dollars And we're actually doing that we actually got that up to a billy's 85 dollars with some Additional increases on the average order value which meant that we could be a lot more profitable on the front end and we worked on kind of back end Opportunities as well, which if you understood the KPI part of the training you'll understand how important that is as well So that's an example here where the total score was 14 out of 20 It's okay, but we still needed to do more testing for stability So that's example number one example number two here is a brand that was doing fitness products This one was really really poor this one I had a coaching call with the business owner and you know his team were running Facebook ads But they just couldn't figure out how this particular Product category was just not performing well and when I had to look at this You know fitness is a highly saturated market, but there was no clear solution or opportunity offered. So this fitness device It was like an abs cruncher so you could kind of wear this Device around your kind of head and upper body Line your back and do some crunches on your stomach, but there's nothing which showed clearly at least from the product positioning What the solution was or new opportunity that people created now if you're in the fitness space? People are looking for the outcome like no one wants to sit there crunching their abs all day That doesn't appeal But if you show that in 30 days you can have this kind of look or you know You can lose this much weight or whatever it is and you have to be careful around policy wording But if you can show the outcome, that's what people are after the offer that they had set up was completely Discount based it was you know 50% off your first order They had some profit through Black Friday and some New Year's sales But then they struggled and you know what we were looking at were what were the ad angles? What were the persuasion techniques news? There's only so far you can take discounting and also the type of users or customers that you generate from discounts Might not be the ones that you really want to work with long term because they came in through a discount

## [09:07 - 12:07]

They'll probably buy again through discount. They may not be the ones that recommend to other people etc etc So they're often needed a lot more work Then you look at the audience and they had good data on Their audiences in Facebook, but there was no market research There was no understanding of what the audience's motivators were their interests the reason for buying they didn't really go deeper into Really understanding the pain points of their customer and trying to build that into into their targeting to find the right people And then into their ads as well and then their conversion their funnel was really poor So overall this client scored 9 out of 20 and our feedback for them and where I actually worked with this Store owner was to work on researching their avatar repositioning their product rebuilding their messaging and their offers the angles and creatives and Tweaking their funnels new landing pages and trying different techniques out to get higher Performance before they start to invest more heavily on Facebook and this thirst third example is an info product Which which is actually one of my products as well We were then looking at right we have this info product How can we make it better? So the actual product that we had The product was serving a real problem Very little competition for this particular product, but maybe a bit too niche So we scored it a four and bear in mind this was graded by my team The offer appealed to a pain point and and relates well to the problems with a narrow nature of messaging It's like missing other people that have the same pain point We knew that because we launched this fast and we wanted to do testing the offer wasn't as strong as it could be So that's something which we knew for this particular product. We could rework then our audiences We had lots of data But we didn't really go deep into understanding the perfect customer and then the funnel was rushed and the funnel was set up really fast and Despite the fact that the product was highly profitable and we were scaling We knew we could squeeze a lot more by working on these different aspects So we score this as a 13 out of 20 with lots of opportunity to do more as well So that gives you an idea of kind of what we're how we use this grading and some of the outcomes Which I will expand out in in subsequent sections on how to improve different parts of your core for as well So at the end of this I want you to kind of tell me what you believe to be your biggest lever if you've gone through this exercise, you've gone through your Advertising core for you've got your scores written down. Be as honest as possible be open to opportunity and this will help you to create your test plan and to come up with

## [12:07 - 15:10]

ideas on How to actually develop your strategy and also before I kind of end this session I want to give you an example of a case study where This core for really helped to kind of define the biggest biggest lever So this example here is an e-commerce store that we took to 16 million in five months and The biggest lever for us was the offer the product was great audience was well defined the funnel is good But it really came down to the offer. So when we did the core for grading Overall this store scored a 17 out of 20 and although the product was great The audience grades funnel was pretty good their biggest opportunity was actually on the offer It wasn't clearly communicating all the possible angles for the product And the emotional reasons why someone would want to buy the functional benefits and we wanted to really work that a lot harder And that for me was one of the key reasons why we were able to Scale this client up. We started off, you know spending $500 a thousand dollars a day It was kind of fairly standard stuff to the point where we scaled it up to I think maybe 3035k a day at really high profitability And the only reason we had to pace our spend was to make sure we could manage stock availability But we did that by really understanding and breaking out what the offer really was So for this particular product and brand it was to make you look and feel your best on the slopes So the product itself was high-tech ski and snowboarding apparel But really the core of the message was making you look and feel great and Kind of look your best on the slopes This really came out in our ads and our creative and I'll show you how to break that down into avatar In the further sessions sections the avatar, which is you know defining who we're targeting We go quite broad initially So it's people interested in ski or snowboard adults 18 to 50 mix of look likes interest and broad So that was the kind of audience that we were targeting then the ads This then helped to build a the kind of theme for the ads So the ads we wanted to get the feeling of looking great and feeling great So our creative and our copy was all tailored around that and then we use the four funnel system Which you'll see later on in the training to build the right ads at the right time for the right user So top of funnel which I call like my cold audience was talking about cool fashion and trendy standout We used influencers then warm is people that have engaged with an ad for example video but not clicked We used in influencers a stylist tall there as well Then hot are my customers that clicked but not purchased we use testimonials kind of reminder messages Dynamic product ads and then existing customers. We used mechanics to try and get them to buy again Which worked really well as well We put all of those together Based on I think we spent two to three weeks ahead of launching the first ad
